title: viburnum pubescens var. longifolium
intro: Viburnum pubescens var. longifolium, also known as downy arrowwood, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and red berries. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of downy arrowwood include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions such as wounds, boils, and eczema. Downy arrowwood can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments to apply topically and take advantage of its medicinal properties.
